summary: WebSocket bug fix tested and VERIFIED. Bug: when updating a status, orders would flash/disappear and only reappear on page reload. Root cause: connectWs depended on fetchOrders which changed on every state change, causing constant WS reconnection. Also, same user received WS echo and triggered refetch. Fix: use refs (fetchOrdersRef, fetchProdRef) for stable WS callback and selfUpdateRef flag to ignore echo from same user. All tests pass - orders remain visible after status updates.

Backend: WebSocket stays open for 3+ seconds - PASS
Backend: WebSocket broadcasts on order create - PASS
Backend: WebSocket broadcasts on order update with type='order_change' - PASS
Backend: WebSocket broadcasts on order move - PASS
Backend: WebSocket broadcasts on order delete - PASS
Backend: WebSocket broadcasts on bulk-move - PASS
Backend: WebSocket message format is JSON with 'type' and 'data' fields - PASS
Frontend: Orders do NOT disappear after status update - PASS
Frontend: Orders remain visible after selecting orders for bulk operations - PASS
Frontend: WebSocket connection is stable (connectWs has empty dependency array) - PASS
Frontend: selfUpdateRef prevents echo from same user - PASS (code review verified)
Frontend: handleCellUpdate sets selfUpdateRef=true before PUT - PASS (code review verified)
Frontend: handleBulkMove sets selfUpdateRef=true before API call - PASS (code review verified)

issues: Existing hydration warnings in console - cosmetic, does not affect functionality
WebSocket initial connection warning during test automation (not reproducible in normal usage)

useOrders.js_lines_134-173.refs_pattern: Uses wsRef, reconnectTimer, fetchOrdersRef, fetchProdRef, selfUpdateRef for stable references
useOrders.js_lines_134-173.connectWs: Has empty dependency array [] - prevents reconnection on state changes
useOrders.js_lines_134-173.ws_message_handler: Checks selfUpdateRef.current before refetching - if true (self update), skips refetch and resets flag
useOrders.js_lines_134-173.cleanup: Properly clears reconnect timer and closes WS on unmount

useOrders.js_lines_184-206.handleCellUpdate: Sets selfUpdateRef.current = true BEFORE PUT call (line 186), provides optimistic update (line 185)
useOrders.js_lines_184-206.handleBulkMove: Sets selfUpdateRef.current = true BEFORE bulk-move call (line 204)

seed_data_creation: Created test order 'WS BUG TEST CLIENT' for testing, moved to trash after tests
context_for_next_testing_agent: WebSocket bug fix verified. The fix uses refs pattern to prevent connectWs from recreating on state changes, and selfUpdateRef flag to skip WS echo from same user. Key files: useOrders.js lines 134-206. Backend WS tests in test_iteration32_websocket.py all pass.

rca_of_the_issue.bug_description: When updating a status field, orders would flash/disappear and only reappear on page reload
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause_1: connectWs had fetchOrders and fetchProductionSummary as dependencies, which changed on every boardFilters/currentBoard state change, causing WebSocket to reconnect constantly
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause_2: Same user who made the update received the WebSocket echo message and triggered a refetch that replaced the optimistically updated orders state
rca_of_the_issue.fix_applied: 1) Used refs (fetchOrdersRef, fetchProdRef) so connectWs has empty dependency array [] and stays stable. 2) Added selfUpdateRef flag - set to true before mutations, checked in WS handler to skip echo from same user
rca_of_the_issue.verification: Frontend tests confirmed orders remain visible (2 before, 2 after, 2 final) after status updates and bulk operations
